---
title: "CISP 360 \n Structured Programming"
subtitle: "Introduction to C++"
author: "Dr. Caleb Fowler"
version: "1.0.0"
# Changelog:
# 1.0.0 (2026-03-07) - Initial generation via gen_lecture prompt.

format:
  revealjs:
    margin: 0.05      # A touch more space on slide
    css: ../styles/styles.css
    theme: serif
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    transition: slide
    incremental: false
    scrollable: false
    controls: true
    hash: true
    history: true
    title-slide: false
    footer: "CISP 360 · Fowler"
    keyboard: true  # Keep this as true to enable the system
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          window.Reveal.on('ready', event => {
            Reveal.configure({
              keyboard: {
                34: 'down',   // Forward button
                33: 'up',     // Back button
                116: 'right',  // Start/Play button
                190: 'left'    // Black Screen button
                79: null        // Disable 'O' for overview
              }
            });
          });
          </script>

engine: jupyter
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: true

resources:
 - topics/**/images/**

---

## Last Time

::: {.incremental}

- The for-each loop and range-based iteration
- Vectors: access, modification, and STL tools
- Parallel vector operations

:::


## Today's Agenda

- What for-each can't do
- 5.1 The While loop
- 5.7 The Do-While loop
- 5.8 The For loop
- 5.9 Which loop to use?
- Code This
- Lab: practicing all three loops

::: notes

:::


{{< include topics/topic_1_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_2_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_3_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_4_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_5_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_6_content.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_7_content.qmd >}}


# Key Takeaways

All three loops share the same three parts: initialize, test, update. The choice between them comes down to one question: do you know the count, must the body run at least once, or neither?


# Next Time

Increment and decrement operators — and how they interact with loops.
